organizations.org: Carmen Llanes Pulido for Mayor of Austin (2024)
organizations.notes: Carmen Llanes Pulido, native East Austinite (donor zip 78722 is East Austin), has been Executive Director of Go Austin/Vamos Austin (GAVA) since March 2017 and ran for Mayor of Austin in 2024, finishing second with about 20% of the vote. Filing employer/occupation ('Go Austin Vamos Austin (GAVA)', 'Executive Director / Nonprofit Executive Director') matches exactly. She previously organized with PODER (People Organized in Defense of Earth and her Resources) in East Austin and ran a fitness/nutrition program at Marathon Kids. FEC search returned no federal PAC contributions under her name; her documented political finance activity is her local Austin mayoral committee (~$71,000 raised through July 2024), which is city-level, not federal. No Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration under 'Llanes' was found. Full bio read of the Austin Monitor profile, InfluenceWatch and UT School of Architecture event bio found extensive local civic and land-use advocacy but no Israel/Palestine, gun, oil-and-gas or defense affiliations on either side of those spectrums.

Ora Houston is the first person to hold the Austin City Council District 1 seat (2015-2019), a lifelong resident of the Blackland neighborhood in East Austin (zip 78722). Occupation 'Retired' and the 78722 zip match her documented residence; her $400 gift to Steven Brown is in the District 1 seat she formerly held. No FEC ideological-PAC contributions and no Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration surfaced.

Reported occupation 'Director' and employer 'PODER' match exactly a well-documented East Austin public-record figure: Susana Almanza co-founded PODER in 1991 and serves as its director. She is a longtime environmental-justice organizer, a two-time Austin City Council District 3 candidate (2014, 2018), and a 2021 appointee to the National Environmental Justice Advisory Council. Her documented advocacy is environmental justice, anti-gentrification and land use in East Austin; no federal PAC, lobbyist-registry, or Israel/Palestine, firearms, oil-and-gas, or defense-industry affiliations were found on either side of those spectrums.

organizations.role: Former Chair of the Board of Directors
organizations.notes: Robin Rather, CEO of Austin-based sustainability consultancy Collective Strength, longtime Austin environmental activist and daughter of newsman Dan Rather. Occupation 'Consultant' / employer 'Self-employed' and zip 78704 match her published FEC contributor records (Robin Rather, CEO, Collective Strength, Austin 78704). Prior career as a tech-sector market research executive at IntelliQuest, CMP Media, and as CEO of Mindwave Research; ~25 years of market research and strategic planning work on 50+ climate, land conservation, water, transit, and regional planning projects. Her all-Democratic federal giving pattern (122 FEC records, lean 1.0) is consistent with her public profile.

organizations.org: Undoing Racism Austin / Undoing White Supremacy Austin
organizations.role: Founder and organizer; trainer with the People's Institute for Survival and Beyond
organizations.notes: D. Lauren Ross, Ph.D., P.E. is the owner of Glenrose Engineering, a woman-owned environmental engineering consultancy at 1405 Hillmont St, Austin TX 78704 — the firm address zip matches the donor zip 78704 exactly, and the occupation/employer strings ('Engineer' / 'Glenrose Engineering') match the firm. Her practice is water-quality protection, groundwater transport, solid waste and environmental litigation. Long public record of Austin environmental advocacy: she provided the technical expertise behind the Save Our Springs ordinance, worked on closure of the Gibraltar hazardous waste site, and opposed the Longhorn oil pipeline. No Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration found and no ideological PAC giving surfaced in FEC searches (pipeline records 34 federal donations, fully Democratic-leaning, but no PAC-level detail was retrievable — the FEC data browser is a JS app and the open FEC API returned HTTP 429).

organizations.org: Texas Campaign for the Environment / TCE Fund
organizations.notes: Austin 78704 and the filing's 'Not Employed' match Robin Schneider, the longtime Executive Director of Texas Campaign for the Environment, who retired in April 2025 after roughly 25 years leading the group's anti-pollution and recycling advocacy (per her organizational bio and a LinkedIn listing her as retired as of April 2025). FEC records for 'Robin Schneider, Austin, TX' with employer 'Texas Campaign for the Environment' and occupation 'Executive Director' shift to 'Not Employed' beginning in late 2025, consistent with that retirement date. A same-named Robin Schneider in Castroville, TX is a different person and not this donor.
